channel.title: Modeling Pre-VLA Robots on HC's Log
channel.description: Recent content in Modeling Pre-VLA Robots on HC's Log

item.title: Basics
item.description:
Diffusion Policy and Flow Matching: Foundations

This note summarizes the foundations of diffusion models, diffusion policy, and flow matching, with an emphasis on both the formal derivation and the intuition behind them.

The central question is:

How can a model learn to transform a simple noise distribution into a complex structured data distribution?

item.title: Diffusion Policy: Visuomotor Policy Learning via Action Diffusion
item.description:
Paper Information

Authors
Cheng Chi, Zhenjia Xu, Siyuan Feng, Eric Cousineau, Yilun Du,
Benjamin Burchfiel, Russ Tedrake, Shuran Song

Conference
Robotics: Science and Systems (RSS)

Year
2023

Paper
https://diffusion-policy.cs.columbia.edu/diffusion_policy_2023.pdf

1. Background and Problem

Research area

Robot learning / visuomotor policy learning.

Core problem

Learning policies that map observations (e.g., images and robot states) to robot actions.

This is challenging because:

  • The action distribution can be multi-modal (multiple valid ways to complete a task).
  • Actions across time are sequentially correlated.
  • Manipulation tasks often require high precision and stable control.

Limitations of previous approaches
